title: River Hydraulics Shallow Water Inversion
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: River Hydraulics Shallow Water Inversion: River hydraulics inversion: infer Manning's roughness and bathymetry from water level and discharge observations. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.swe.saint_venant…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~80; bank_overflow_uncertainty, sediment_transport_coupling set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: dA/dt + dQ/dx = q_lat; dQ/dt + d(Q ** 2/A)/dx + g*A*dh/dx + g*A*S_f = 0; S_f = n ** 2*Q ** 2/(A ** 2*R^{4/3})
E.world_state_x: Manning's roughness n(x) and cross-section bathymetry A(h, x) along river reach
E.observation_y: water stage H(t) at N_gauges gauging stations and discharge Q(t)

W.regime: Existence of the recovered Manning_roughness_bathymetry is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 80); bank_overflow_uncertainty dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: stage RMSE < 0.1 m for calibrated HEC-RAS model. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~80. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |bank_overflow_uncertainty|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_cross_sections * N_timesteps) for 1D routing per iteration
